past_medical_history: No known prior medical history — patient has avoided medical care for over 10 years
family_history: Father with colon cancer diagnosed at 68, deceased at 74
No family history of lymphoma, leukemia, or brain tumors

social_history.summary: Retired postal worker (retired 2 years ago). Lives alone. Social history obtained reluctantly: 15-year period of unprotected sexual contact with multiple male partners (disclosed under gentle questioning). Has not been tested for HIV or STIs. Reports 8 kg unintentional weight loss over 4 months. Intermittent fevers and night sweats. Former smoker (quit 10 years ago, 15 pack-years). Occasional alcohol use (2-3 beers per week, none recently due to feeling unwell). No IV drug use.

neurological_exam.mental_status: GCS 14 (E4V4M6). Alert but mildly confused — oriented to person and place but not date. Slow to respond to questions. Affect flat. Impaired attention (unable to perform serial 7s, makes errors on months of the year backwards). Short-term memory impaired (recalls 1/3 objects at 5 minutes).
neurological_exam.cranial_nerves: CN II: Subtle bilateral papilledema on fundoscopic exam. Visual fields full to confrontation. CN III-XII otherwise intact. Mild left facial droop (central pattern, upper motor neuron type).
neurological_exam.motor: Left arm pronator drift present. Left hand fine motor impairment (difficulty with rapid alternating movements and finger tapping). Left grip strength 4/5, rest of left upper extremity 4+/5. Right upper extremity 5/5. Lower extremities 5/5 bilaterally. Normal tone throughout.
neurological_exam.sensory: Intact to light touch, pinprick, vibration, and proprioception in all extremities. No sensory neglect.
neurological_exam.reflexes: 2+ and symmetric throughout upper and lower extremities. Plantar responses flexor bilaterally.
neurological_exam.coordination: Mild left arm dysmetria on finger-to-nose testing. Right side normal. Heel-to-shin normal bilaterally.
neurological_exam.gait: Mildly unsteady, tends to veer left. Tandem gait impaired.
neurological_exam.special_signs: Oral thrush present on examination. Generalized lymphadenopathy palpable (cervical, axillary bilateral). Mild left facial droop. Bilateral papilledema.

patient.chief_complaint: Progressive headache and confusion over 3 weeks
patient.history_present_illness: 56-year-old right-handed African American male presenting with 3-week progressive headache, difficulty concentrating, personality change (more withdrawn and flat), and mild left arm clumsiness. He was initially evaluated at an outside hospital where a CT head revealed an enhancing brain lesion, and he was transferred for neurosurgical evaluation. He was referred with a preliminary diagnosis of 'brain tumor, likely GBM.' On further history, he reports 8 kg weight loss over 4 months, intermittent fevers (not measured at home), and night sweats. He admits to a 15-year history of unprotected sex with multiple male partners. He has not seen a doctor in over 10 years and has never been tested for HIV. On physical examination, oral thrush is noted and generalized lymphadenopathy is palpable.

findings.location: Right frontal lobe, extending to corpus callosum and abutting the right lateral ventricle. Subependymal spread along the ventricular margins.

signal_characteristics.T1: Isointense to slightly hypointense to gray matter
signal_characteristics.DWI: Restricted diffusion throughout the mass (hypercellular). Diffusion restriction is DIFFUSE and HOMOGENEOUS — not rim-restricted as in GBM or abscess.
signal_characteristics.post_gadolinium: Homogeneous enhancement — NOT ring-enhancing. The mass enhances uniformly throughout without central necrosis. This is an important distinction from GBM, which typically shows ring enhancement with central necrosis.

findings.mass_effect: Moderate perilesional vasogenic edema. 6 mm leftward midline shift. Partial effacement of right frontal horn.
findings.borders: Moderately well-defined but with subependymal spread along ventricular surface

additional_observations: HOMOGENEOUS enhancement pattern (NOT ring-enhancing) — important distinction from GBM
No central necrosis (GBM typically has central necrosis)
DIFFUSE restricted diffusion (hypercellularity) — GBM typically shows rim restriction only
mri.impression: 2 cm homogeneously enhancing periventricular mass in the right frontal lobe with subependymal spread and corpus callosum involvement. The HOMOGENEOUS enhancement pattern (without central necrosis) and diffuse DWI restriction are atypical for GBM and raise concern for primary CNS lymphoma, particularly in the appropriate clinical context. 6 mm leftward midline shift with moderate vasogenic edema. Clinical correlation recommended.

background.posterior_dominant_rhythm: 7-8 Hz theta rhythm (mildly slow for age)
background.symmetry: Asymmetric — more prominent right frontal polymorphic delta activity

findings.morphology: 1-3 Hz polymorphic delta activity, maximal right frontal

eeg.impression: No epileptiform discharges. Mildly slow background. Clinical correlation recommended.

labs.interpretation: Abnormal values: WBC 3.4 x10^9/L (L), Hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL (L), Hematocrit 33.8 % (L), Platelets 148 x10^9/L (L), Lymphocytes 0.6 x10^9/L (L), ESR 52 mm/hr (H), CRP 28 mg/L (H), LDH 320 U/L (H), HIV-1/2 Ag/Ab combination (4th gen) Reactive  (H), HIV-1 viral load 156000 copies/mL (H), CD4 count 82 cells/mcL (L), CD4 percentage 6 % (L), Toxoplasma IgG Positive  (H), EBV DNA PCR (blood) 12400 copies/mL (H).
abnormal_values_summary: HIV-1 positive with viral load 156,000 copies/mL (NEW DIAGNOSIS)

findings.signal: choline: Markedly elevated (Cho/Cr ratio 3.8); NAA: Markedly reduced; creatine: Mildly reduced; lipid_lactate: Elevated lipid and lactate peaks; amino_acids: No amino acid peaks identified; 2HG: Absent (no 2-hydroxyglutarate peak — argues against IDH-mutant glioma)
findings.description: MR spectroscopy of the enhancing mass

output.impression: Tissue diagnosis is required. Clinical correlation recommended.

signal_characteristics.rCBV: Moderate elevation: maximum rCBV ratio 2.4 relative to contralateral normal white matter
signal_characteristics.perfusion_pattern: Relatively homogeneous moderate perfusion elevation throughout the mass

additional_observations: rCBV of 2.4 is LOWER than typical for GBM (which usually has rCBV >4.0 due to neoangiogenesis)
PCNSL typically shows moderate rCBV elevation (2.0-3.0) due to moderate vascularity without true neoangiogenesis
GBM typically shows markedly elevated rCBV (>4.0) with heterogeneous perfusion
output.impression: MR perfusion demonstrates moderate rCBV elevation (2.4), which is LOWER than expected for GBM (typically >4.0). Combined with the homogeneous enhancement pattern and periventricular location, the perfusion findings favor PCNSL over GBM. Clinical correlation recommended.

output.opening_pressure: 24 cmH2O (mildly elevated, consistent with mass effect)

special_studies.value: Large atypical lymphoid cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, prominent nucleoli, irregular nuclear contours. Consistent with malignant lymphocytes.
special_studies.clinical_significance: Positive cytology — malignant lymphoid cells identified in CSF

special_studies.value: Monoclonal B-cell population identified: CD20+, CD19+, CD10-, BCL6+, MUM1+. Kappa light chain restricted.
special_studies.clinical_significance: Monoclonal B-cell population with kappa restriction confirms B-cell lymphoma. CD20+/BCL6+/MUM1+ profile is consistent with non-germinal center type DLBCL.
EBV-associated lymphoma. In the setting of AIDS, EBV drives virtually all PCNSL.
Markedly elevated CSF EBV DNA, strongly supporting EBV-driven PCNSL

output.interpretation: Opening pressure: 24 cmH2O. WBC: N/A (N/A PMN/N/A lymph).
output.clinical_correlation: CSF findings are diagnostic for primary CNS lymphoma. Stereotactic biopsy may still be pursued for definitive tissue confirmation, but CSF cytology and flow cytometry provide strong evidence for PCNSL.

Neuropathology.value: Dense sheets of large atypical lymphoid cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, prominent nucleoli, and brisk mitotic activity. Characteristic angiocentric (perivascular) distribution pattern.

output.interpretation: Abnormal values: Histology Dense sheets of large atypical lymphoid cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, prominent nucleoli, and brisk mitotic activity. Characteristic angiocentric (perivascular) distribution pattern.  (H), CD20 (immunohistochemistry) Positive (diffuse strong)  (H), CD79a Positive  (H), BCL6 Positive  (H), MUM1/IRF4 Positive  (H), Ki-67 proliferation index >90%  (H), EBV EBER ISH Positive (in virtually all tumor cells)  (H).
abnormal_values_summary: DIAGNOSIS: EBV-associated diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (PCNSL) (H)

PET_CT_Body.value: Intensely FDG-avid right frontal periventricular mass (SUVmax 22). No other CNS lesions.
Mildly FDG-avid bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 3.2). Pattern consistent with reactive lymphadenopathy.
Mildly FDG-avid bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 2.8). Reactive pattern.
No FDG-avid masses. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy above reactive threshold.
No FDG-avid masses. Spleen mildly enlarged (14 cm) with mildly increased uptake. No hepatic lesions. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

output.interpretation: Abnormal values: Brain Intensely FDG-avid right frontal periventricular mass (SUVmax 22). No other CNS lesions.  (H), Cervical lymph nodes Mildly FDG-avid bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 3.2). Pattern consistent with reactive lymphadenopathy.  (H), Axillary lymph nodes Mildly FDG-avid bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 2.8). Reactive pattern.  (H), Abdomen/Pelvis No FDG-avid masses. Spleen mildly enlarged (14 cm) with mildly increased uptake. No hepatic lesions. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.  (H).
abnormal_values_summary: Intensely FDG-avid right frontal brain mass (SUVmax 22)
No systemic lymphoma — confirms PRIMARY CNS lymphoma

ground_truth.primary_diagnosis: Primary CNS lymphoma (EBV-associated DLBCL) in the setting of undiagnosed HIV/AIDS

differential.key_distinguishing: Homogeneous enhancement without central necrosis is atypical for GBM (which typically ring-enhances with necrosis). Periventricular location with subependymal spread favors lymphoma. rCBV 2.4 is lower than typical GBM (>4.0). Positive CSF cytology with monoclonal B-cells confirms lymphoma. GFAP negative on biopsy excludes glial tumor.

Toxoplasma IgG positive indicating prior exposure, and CD4 82 puts patient at risk. However, single homogeneous mass (not typical multiple ring-enhancing lesions of toxo). Toxoplasma PCR negative in CSF. Biopsy confirms lymphoma, not toxoplasmosis.

Homogeneous enhancement is not ring-enhancing pattern of abscess. DWI restriction is diffuse and homogeneous (abscess shows CENTRAL restricted diffusion in the cavity). No fever or significant leukocytosis to suggest pyogenic abscess.

No systemic malignancy on whole-body PET-CT. Periventricular location and subependymal spread are atypical for metastasis. Single lesion with this imaging pattern in an immunocompromised patient favors PCNSL.

optimal_actions.action: Test for HIV immediately based on clinical features (oral thrush, lymphadenopathy, weight loss, leukopenia with lymphopenia)
optimal_actions.expected_finding: HIV positive with CD4 82 (AIDS) and viral load 156,000

optimal_actions.action: Order brain MRI with gadolinium for detailed lesion characterization
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Homogeneously enhancing periventricular mass — pattern favoring lymphoma over GBM

optimal_actions.action: Order MR perfusion to distinguish PCNSL (moderate rCBV) from GBM (high rCBV)
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Moderate rCBV (2.4) favoring lymphoma over GBM

optimal_actions.action: Obtain CSF cytology and flow cytometry BEFORE initiating corticosteroids (steroids lyse lymphoma cells causing 'vanishing tumor')
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Monoclonal B-cells with CD20+/BCL6+/MUM1+ confirming DLBCL

optimal_actions.action: If CSF non-diagnostic, perform stereotactic biopsy (NOT craniotomy for resection)
Order whole-body PET-CT to exclude systemic lymphoma
optimal_actions.expected_finding: No systemic lymphoma — confirms primary CNS lymphoma

optimal_actions.action: Refer to neuro-oncology for high-dose methotrexate-based chemotherapy
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Treatment plan for PCNSL (NOT GBM protocol)

optimal_actions.action: Initiate antiretroviral therapy (ART) for HIV after oncologic treatment plan is established, in coordination with infectious disease
optimal_actions.expected_finding: ART initiation with monitoring for immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS)

critical_actions: Test for HIV immediately — lymphadenopathy, oral thrush, leukopenia, and weight loss demand it
DO NOT proceed directly to craniotomy/resection — PCNSL should NOT be debulked surgically
Obtain CSF cytology and flow cytometry before initiating steroids (steroids cause 'vanishing lymphoma' and prevent diagnosis)
Stereotactic biopsy for tissue diagnosis if CSF is non-diagnostic
Avoid corticosteroids before biopsy if at all possible (they lyse lymphoma cells and can prevent diagnosis)
Start ART for HIV after oncologic treatment plan established
Refer to neuro-oncology for high-dose methotrexate-based chemotherapy
contraindicated_actions: Craniotomy for tumor resection (PCNSL is treated with chemotherapy, not surgery — surgical debulking provides no survival benefit and delays definitive treatment)
Administering dexamethasone before obtaining tissue/CSF diagnosis (vanishing tumor phenomenon — steroids lyse lymphoma cells, making diagnosis impossible)
Empirically treating for toxoplasmosis without tissue confirmation in a single-mass presentation (classic toxo presents with multiple ring-enhancing lesions)
Initiating temozolomide (GBM protocol) without confirming the diagnosis — temozolomide is ineffective against PCNSL
key_reasoning_points: Homogeneous enhancement with periventricular location and subependymal spread is the classic MRI pattern of PCNSL — GBM typically shows ring-enhancement with central necrosis
Lower rCBV (2.4) than expected for GBM (typically >4.0) suggests a less vascular tumor like lymphoma rather than the neoangiogenic GBM
Leukopenia + oral thrush + generalized lymphadenopathy + weight loss + fevers in a sexually active man with multiple male partners = undiagnosed HIV/AIDS must be tested
CD4 count of 82 makes PCNSL and CNS toxoplasmosis the top differential diagnoses in AIDS with a brain mass
Elevated EBV DNA in blood (12,400) and CSF (45,000) strongly correlates with EBV-driven PCNSL in AIDS
CSF cytology showing monoclonal B-cells with CD20+/BCL6+/MUM1+ and kappa restriction confirms DLBCL
PCNSL management is fundamentally different from GBM: high-dose methotrexate-based chemotherapy + whole-brain radiation (in AIDS, with concurrent ART), NOT surgical resection and temozolomide
Corticosteroids given before biopsy can cause the tumor to 'vanish' temporarily, preventing tissue diagnosis

red_herrings.data_point: Enhancing brain mass with midline shift referred as 'likely GBM'
red_herrings.intended_effect: Immediate assumption of GBM because it is the most common primary brain tumor, leading to surgical planning
red_herrings.correct_interpretation: Enhancement pattern matters: HOMOGENEOUS enhancement (PCNSL) is different from RING enhancement with necrosis (GBM). Periventricular location with subependymal spread strongly favors PCNSL.

red_herrings.intended_effect: In an AIDS patient with a brain mass, suggests CNS toxoplasmosis requiring empiric treatment with pyrimethamine-sulfadiazine
red_herrings.correct_interpretation: Toxoplasma IgG indicates prior exposure, which is present in 30-50% of adults. A single homogeneous mass (not multiple ring-enhancing lesions) is atypical for CNS toxoplasmosis. CSF Toxoplasma PCR is negative, and biopsy confirms lymphoma.

red_herrings.intended_effect: May suggest high-grade glioma to the reader accustomed to seeing DWI restriction in GBM
red_herrings.correct_interpretation: Both GBM and PCNSL show restricted diffusion, but the pattern differs: PCNSL shows DIFFUSE homogeneous restriction (dense cellularity throughout), while GBM shows rim restriction around necrotic center. The diffuse pattern here favors PCNSL.

metadata.diagnostic_challenge: Primary CNS lymphoma in an undiagnosed HIV/AIDS patient, initially referred as 'likely GBM.' Requires recognition of (1) clinical features of undiagnosed HIV, (2) imaging features distinguishing PCNSL from GBM (homogeneous vs ring enhancement, moderate vs high rCBV), and (3) fundamentally different management (chemotherapy, not surgery). Corticosteroids must be avoided before tissue diagnosis.
